passage_text:
The International Olympiad in Informatics (IOI) 2026 Scientific Committee invites the IOI community to help design the competition tasks. IOI tasks focus on efficient, correct algorithms (算法) with simple input and output. IOI needs creative tasks that require insight and allow multiple approaches. The nature of past tasks should not restrict the design of new tasks. We are particularly interested in tasks with accessible basic rules and tasks that illustrate algorithms arising from real-life situations.
Requirements
·Be secret to potential IOI 2026 contestants
·Not used in any recent similar competition
·Solvable during an IOI round
·Clear, easy-to-understand statements
·Original and/ or innovative
Task Criteria
·Statement in English
·Description of the full-score algorithm
·Author contact and background: Name, email, country, and roles in IOI or national Olympiad (IOI 2024-2026)
Submission Process & Method
·Pack all materials into a single file (.zip or .tgz for multiple files) and submit online.
·Authorization Code: samarkand
·Encryption (加密): do not encrypt on your own; the upload page handles it automatically.
What Happens Next
A shortlist of around 10 tasks will be selected, six of which are expected to be used at IOI 2026 in Uzbekistan.
Authors of shortlisted tasks may be invited to attend IOI 2026 as guests (travel typically at the author’s expense; accommodations covered by the host).
Authors of tasks included in the official competition will be credited on the IOI website.
Deadline: March 31, 2026, 23:59 UTC+5
Contact Information: hsc@ioi2026.uz
question_text: What type of tasks is given special priority to by the committee?
stem: What type of tasks is given special priority to by the committee?

options.label: C
options.text: Highly demanding tasks for top participants.

options.label: D
options.text: Tasks closely following past competition patterns.

explanation: QA support states that the committee is particularly interested in tasks with accessible basic rules and algorithms arising from real-life situations.

question_text: What is a must for a task submission?
stem: What is a must for a task submission?

options.label: A
options.text: Being encrypted by hand before uploading.

options.label: B
options.text: Applying accessible basic rules of algorithms.

options.label: C
options.text: Being kept from potential IOI 2026 contestants.

options.label: D
options.text: Providing solutions in the author’s native language.

explanation: QA support cites the Requirements item that tasks must be secret to potential IOI 2026 contestants.
